title: Extreme Cold Warming Centers - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Extreme Cold Warming Centers 114 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness 211INFO WINTER AND SEVERE WEATHER INFORMATION SERVICES MULTNOMAH COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Seasonal winter shelters may be open in addition to severe cold weather shelters that may be activated when a severe weather notice is issued by Homeless Service Department (HSD). The criteria used to determine a severe weather notice in Multnomah County and the corresponding opening of emergency warming centers are: -Temperatures forecast at 25°F or below OR -Forecasts predict at least an inch of snow in most areas OR -Overnight temperatures forecast at 32°F or below, with at least an inch of driving rain OR -Other conditions, including severe wind chills or extreme temperature fluctuations Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Winter shelters are open throughout the winter season, and severe weather shelters may be activated during times of severe weather between November 1-March 31 each year. Hours vary. Email 211 Visit Website More Details COMPASS OUTREACH COLD WEATHER SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a warming center when temperatures drop to 36 degrees. Partners with Faith Lutheran Church located at 2741 Sherman Avenue, North Bend, OR 97459. 1200 Newmark Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Unrestricted; priority is given to people experiencing homelessness with mental health conditions Hours: 7 days per week 6pm-8am when temperatures are 36 degrees or colder Email (541) 888-3202 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER BENTON COUNTY ALSEA COMMUNITY LIBRARY DAYTIME WINTER WARMING CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in day space to go inside out of the cold to warm up temporarily throughout the winter season. Provides wifi, public bathrooms, and water refill station. Additional services may be offered. 19192 Alsea Highway Alsea, OR 97324 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Pets allowed but must be kept in crates. Crates are available to borrow if needed.
